title: Corporate Profit Growth Returns
text:
Corporate Profit Growth Returns

Thomas Gatley

Gavekal Dragonomics | 23 Jun 2026

After years of little growth, China’s corporate profits have started off 2026 with an abrupt surge, thanks to a combination of the global investment boom in artificial intelligence and windfall earnings from the energy-price shock during the Iran war. The AI boom seems set to continue, but the profit bonanza in the oil and gas industry will soon end. While domestic demand remains weak on any measure, even Chinese companies outside those booming sectors will continue to enjoy strong sales abroad. The net result is that growth in China’s corporate profits will cool off in the second half of the year, but will still come in stronger this year.Chinese companies enjoyed a phenomenal two-year run in 2021 and 2022: mainland and Hong Kong-listed industrial and services-sector firms—numbering about 5,000 in total, excluding financial and real-estate companies—posted 40% revenue growth, while sales among the roughly half a million firms included in the official industrial survey rose 30%. But that pace slowed to a crawl from 2023 to 2025, rising by only about 2-3% per year.
